title: Khandoba Temple, Jejuri
tagline: Khandoba Temple, Jejuri — spiritual town of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Khandoba Temple of Jejuri is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Khandoba, located on a hill in the town of Jejuri, Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: The Khandoba Temple of Jejuri is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Khandoba, located on a hill in the town of Jejuri, Maharashtra, India. It is one of the most prominent Hindu pilgrimage centres of Maharashtra. Jejuri's Khandoba is a Kuladevata of many farming families, Brahmins and nomadic Dhangar tribe of the Maharashtra and Deccan region. According to legends and folklore, Khandoba was a human Avatar of Bhagawan Shiva; he used to live and rule the region from Jejuri-gad (transl. Jejuri fort), where the Mandir is now present.

topPlaces.name: Jejuri
topPlaces.description: Jejuri (Marathi pronunciation: [d͡ʒed͡zuɾiː]) is a city and a municipal council in the Pune district of Maharashtra, India. Khandoba Mandir is an important Hindu temple to the Hindu Lord Khandoba, one of the most visited tirtha (holy places) in Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Nazare Dam
topPlaces.description: Nazare Dam, also called Malharsagar, is an earthfill dam on Karha river near Jejuri, Pune district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Veer Dam
topPlaces.description: The Veer Dam is one of the important dams in Maharashtra, India. It is a rubble-concrete dam constructed on Nira River.

items.note: Jejuri (Marathi pronunciation: [d͡ʒed͡zuɾiː]) is a city and a municipal council in the Pune district of Maharashtra, India. Khandoba Mandir is an important Hindu temple to the Hindu Lord Khandoba, one of the most visited tirtha (holy places) in Maharashtra.
Nazare Dam, also called Malharsagar, is an earthfill dam on Karha river near Jejuri, Pune district in the state of Maharashtra in India.
The Veer Dam is one of the important dams in Maharashtra, India. It is a rubble-concrete dam constructed on Nira River.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Khandoba Temple, Jejuri?
faq.a: The best time to visit Khandoba Temple, Jejuri is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Khandoba Temple, Jejuri?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (42 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Khandoba Temple, Jejuri?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Khandoba Temple, Jejuri at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Khandoba Temple, Jejuri?
faq.a: Stays in Khandoba Temple, Jejuri start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Khandoba Temple, Jejuri?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jejuri, Nazare Dam, Veer Dam.
